name: config-plugin-example-check
summary: Deterministically validates config plugin examples in the Expo docs against the real plugin option types in the monorepo's packages/.

source_of_truth.primary: The TypeScript option type behind each plugin's exported ConfigPlugin<T> (or the option parameter of the plugin's default-export function), read with the TypeScript compiler.
source_of_truth.entry: The canonical entry that app.plugin.js loads (e.g. require('./plugin/build/withX') -> plugin/src/withX.ts), tried before plugin/src/index.ts so a stub index.ts cannot mask the real options. Import aliases on the traced plugin identifier are resolved across files.
source_of_truth.why_not_typedoc: The generated docs TypeDoc JSON only emits plugin option types for plugins that re-export them from their plugin entrypoint, so its coverage is near-empty. Reading source directly covers every first-party plugin.
source_of_truth.schema_utils: Where a plugin ships an @expo/schema-utils schema it is declared as JSONSchema<OptionType>, so it cannot diverge from the TS type; the type is sufficient and authoritative.

scope.versions: unversioned + the latest detected vNN.0.0 dir. Override with --versions.
scope.old_versions_excluded: packages/ reflects the current SDK only; validating frozen v50-v55 against current types would manufacture false drift.
scope.discovery: <ConfigPluginExample> blocks plus any ```json/```json5 block containing a plugins array, within the scoped version dirs.

verdicts: validated-type  - checked against the extracted TS option type
heuristic-only  - third-party plugin (no monorepo source); quoted-boolean smell check only
no-options      - plugin used with no options object, or plugin that accepts no options
unparseable     - block is not valid JSON5 after normalizing {{ }} template variables (often an intentional type sketch)
unresolved      - first-party plugin whose option type could not be extracted (reason printed; NOT fact-checked)

severities.warning: unknown-prop (incl. options passed to a no-option plugin), heuristic-quoted-boolean, malformed-json (a <ConfigPluginExample> block that is not valid JSON and is not a type sketch or intentional partial).
severities.info: unparseable (type sketches / partial raw blocks), unresolved.

resolution_strategies: entry = app.plugin.js require target (build->src), tried before plugin/src/index.ts; first entry that yields options wins
export default <ident> / createRunOncePlugin(<ident>, ...) -> trace ident's declaration, resolving import aliases across files
ConfigPlugin<T> annotation on the traced variable -> T
last function parameter not named 'config' (covers the (config, props) ConfigPlugin shape and the docs-facing (props) wrapper shape)
fallback: first ConfigPlugin<...> annotation in the entry file
known_limitations: Third-party plugins get a heuristic quoted-boolean check only.
Type-sketch examples (bare type names as values) are reported as unparseable, not validated.
A first-party plugin with a new export shape may report unresolved until findOptionTypeNode/propsTypeFromDecl in scripts/check.ts is taught the shape.
A plugin that genuinely forwards an option only via a nested level the docs use at top level (or vice versa) is judged at the level the type declares it.

exit_codes.1: at least one type-mismatch or invalid-enum
exit_codes.2: fatal setup error (docs/packages path or typescript not found)
